title: The Window Small Brands Have Right Now
meta: Vertical 9:16 reel, ~27s. Featured brand: Everlane. Inspired by mobileeditingclub (DNn-EILzpWj). Build-it-in-Masonry walkthrough.
concept: A build-it-in-Masonry walkthrough for Everlane. The finished reel looks like a six-figure fashion campaign for a small D2C brand, but every frame is generated in Masonry by following the 'Make in Masonry' step under each beat. The story: small brands feel the content struggle (perfect quality, constant posting, juggling products and staging), then the reveal lands, that whole campaign was made in Masonry, by you, right now, before the big players catch up. The app-screen beats (products, staging, content grid) are screen recordings of Masonry itself, so the viewer literally sees the tool that produced the images.
audio: Atmospheric, cinematic instrumental that builds steadily. Audio-only: no voiceover. Single words and short phrases appear cut by cut at an editorial pace, every cut on the beat.

beats.visual: Opening portrait: model in neutral tones in a wheat field, soft golden-hour backlight. Single word. Scroll-stopping hook.
beats.masonry: Upload Everlane's linen blazer flat-lay to the Masonry product library. Prompt: 'editorial portrait, model wearing this blazer, wheat field, soft golden-hour backlight, vertical 9:16.' Generate, pick the strongest frame. This is your opener.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 0: 'what' single-word opener over a first editorial portrait.

beats.visual: Tighter portrait, same model in the structured blazer. The phrase begins to form.
beats.masonry: In Masonry hit 'Vary' on the opener or prompt 'closer crop, neutral studio tone.' Masonry holds the same face and garment, so the cut reads as one continuous shoot.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 1: 'create images' on a second editorial portrait.

beats.visual: Wide shot: model in an Everlane oversized wool coat walking a clean city street. Full campaign quality.
beats.masonry: Drop Everlane's wool coat into Masonry. Prompt 'full-length, model walking, clean city street, overcast editorial light.' Save this generation as a style reference so the later shots match it.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 2: 'like these' on a wide fashion coat shot.

beats.visual: Four-model editorial group shot, all in coordinated Everlane basics. The aspiration: this for your whole line.
beats.masonry: Load the coat, blazer and linen set into the product library and set the Everlane brand kit (palette + type). Prompt one 'four-model group editorial, coordinated neutral basics.' One prompt styles the whole line together, on brand.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 3: 'for your products' on a multi-model group editorial shot.

beats.visual: Single word over a wide, dramatic group shot, models staggered and looking away.
beats.masonry: Re-roll the group with 'staggered poses, looking away, dramatic wide.' Pick the most editorial frame. No reshoot, no second casting call, just another Masonry generation.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 4: 'yes,' on the wide dramatic editorial group cut.

beats.visual: Same energy, tight on confident faces. The reveal lands: every shot so far came from one tool.
beats.masonry: Reveal beat. Optional 0.3s screen-record of the Masonry Generate button firing, cut in right as the 'with Masonry' text hits, so the viewer connects every prior frame to the tool.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 5: 'yes, with AI' confirmation cut.

beats.visual: Cut to a solo model, seated, more intimate. The reel shifts from aspiration to empathy.
beats.masonry: Prompt 'single model seated, intimate, soft window light, Everlane knit.' Masonry reuses the same brand face from the earlier shots so it reads as the same campaign, not a new model.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 6: 'if you're a small brand' intimate portrait shift.

beats.visual: Close-up of fabric texture, Everlane cotton, close enough to feel. The struggle is real, the product is real.
beats.masonry: Upload the real Everlane Japanese-cotton swatch and prompt 'extreme macro, fabric weave, raking light.' Masonry renders the actual product texture, your proof it is the real product, not generic AI fabric.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 7: 'you know the struggle' tactile fabric close-up.

beats.visual: Editorial model in a classic Everlane trench, tack-sharp outdoor light. The standard is high.
beats.masonry: Everlane trench from the product library. Prompt 'editorial portrait, classic trench, tack-sharp outdoor light.' Pick the crispest generation, quality is the message on this beat.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 8: 'Perfect Quality.' on the editorial coat portrait.

beats.visual: Fashion jacket close-up in warm brown tones, confident posture. Showing up every day.
beats.masonry: Use Masonry's Restyle on the previous shot to shift the palette warm ('warm brown tones, jacket detail') without regenerating the whole frame, so the model stays consistent.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 9: 'while showing up' jacket close-up.

beats.visual: Wide lifestyle shot: Everlane product draped in a minimal sunlit room. Everything at once.
beats.masonry: Place the product in a styled set: prompt 'product draped over chair, minimal sunlit room.' Same product, lifestyle context, generated rather than shot on location.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 10: 'and juggling' product-in-context wide shot.

beats.ost: products. staging. thousands of content pieces.
beats.visual: Three cuts. sec-11: Masonry product library showing Everlane's catalog. sec-12: Masonry staging screen, one product selected, prompt typed. sec-13: hold on a dark-jacket editorial with 'thousands' text.
beats.masonry: These are the Masonry-UI beats, screen-record the real app. sec-11: the product library with Everlane's full catalog loaded. sec-12: the staging screen with one product selected and a prompt typed in. sec-13: cut to a finished generation with the 'thousands' text. This is you literally showing the tool that makes the reel.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 11-13: app UI showing products, a staging screen, then a 'thousands' editorial cut.

beats.visual: Masonry output gallery: a grid of finished Everlane content tiles, all consistent and on brand.
beats.masonry: Screen-record Masonry's output gallery, a grid of finished Everlane assets generated from the same brand kit. This is the 'one tool, every asset' payoff shot.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 14-15: 'content' social grid content UI.

beats.ost: here's the good news: it is for you.
beats.visual: Pivot. Grid scrolls, then a stark black typography card 'here's the good,' then a wide editorial model looking into frame as 'is' and 'for' build.
beats.masonry: Typography beats: build the text cards in your editor over a clean Masonry backdrop. For the 'is/for' frame, generate a model looking straight into camera in Masonry and lay the text on top.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 16-19: 'the / here's the good / is / for' pivot sequence.

beats.ost: this is the window. before the big players catch up.
beats.visual: Four ethereal, dreamy cuts: a minimal 'this' typography frame, a misty fashion editorial, a soft-light face close-up, a waiting portrait. Quiet urgency.
beats.masonry: Prompt 'misty, dreamy editorial, soft diffusion, model through haze' and batch-generate 3-4 variations in one go. Seed-lock keeps them feeling like one series, use them as the atmospheric run under the text.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 20-22: 'this / this is the window / waiting' ethereal editorial sequence.

beats.visual: Close: dramatic wide fashion shot, Everlane product card on brand background, Masonry wordmark title card, then the model walking away in a hero outfit. Clean, confident.
beats.masonry: Close on a hero Masonry generation of the model walking away, then your Everlane-plus-Masonry end card. Optional 0.5s Masonry-logo screen-record to seal that the whole reel was made in it.
beats.mirrors: Inspiration sec 22-26: 'use / with / question / you' product card, brand title, and walking-model close.

notes: Editor workflow: (1) Load Everlane's real products (blazer, coat, trench, linen set, cotton swatch) into the Masonry product library and set the brand kit (palette + type). (2) Generate each beat with the 'Make in Masonry' prompt under it, pick the hero frame, and use Vary and Restyle to keep the model and garments consistent across shots. (3) Screen-record the Masonry UI for the products, staging and content-grid beats. (4) Assemble in your editor to the music, drop the on-screen text per beat, audio-only with no voiceover. Everlane is a stand-in demo brand: swap in the real client and their products when producing. No media has been generated yet, generation is gated on separate approval per project policy.
